title: Tangrakottai
tagline: Tangrakottai — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Thenkaraikottai is a ruined castle was built by Seelappa Nayakkar and Chennappa Nayakkar of Vijayanagar dynasty.
overview.description: Thenkaraikottai is a ruined castle was built by Seelappa Nayakkar and Chennappa Nayakkar of Vijayanagar dynasty. This castle is the only one built on land and not on a hill like others and is situated near Pappireddipatti, Dharmapuri district, Tamil Nadu, India. The Major attraction of the place is the Sri Kalyana Ramaswamy temple which is dedicated to lord Rama (avatar of vishnu) with beautiful architecture is present in centre of the fort. There are few ruined historic buildings which includes Granaries, stables for elephants and horses, cannons, a bathing area for the princes and many more.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Laligam
topPlaces.description: Laligam is a village panchayat in the Dharmapuri district, Tamil Nadu, India. As of 2011 Laligam had a population of 7,601 persons.

topPlaces.name: Poolapatti
topPlaces.description: Poolapatti, is a village also officially spelled as Poolappatti, is a village located 6 km east from the major town Nallampalli, Tamil Nadu, India. This village comes under the Mittareddihalli (M.Reddihalli) Gram panchayat.

topPlaces.name: St. Francis Xavier Church, Kovilur
topPlaces.description: The Saint Francis Xavier Church in Kovilur, Dharamapuri is the oldest parish church in the diocese. It is called the mother church and the first Parish in the entire diocese.

topPlaces.name: Kovilur, Dharmapuri
topPlaces.description: Kovilur is a small Village in Nallampalli Block in Dharmapuri district of Tamil Nadu State, India. It comes under Nallampalli Panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Chenraya Perumal Temple
topPlaces.description: Chenraya Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple located in the town of Adhiyamankottam, 7 km from Dharmapuri on the Salem road. The temple is dedicated to Vishnu and is believed to have been constructed by Hoysala and Vijayanagar kings, the most prominent being Krishnadevaraya.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Dharmapuri
topPlaces.description: The Diocese of Dharmapuri (Latin: Dharmapurien(sis)) is a Latin diocese of the Catholic Church located in the city of Dharmapuri, India, in the ecclesiastical province of Pondicherry and Cuddalore. The diocese administers to the political region of Dharmapuri district and Krishnagiri district.

topPlaces.name: Nallampalli
topPlaces.description: Nallampalli is a Census town in Dharmapuri district in Tamil Nadu, India. It is the headquarters of Nallampalli taluk.

topPlaces.name: Dharmapuri
topPlaces.description: Dharmapuri is a town in the north western part of Tamil Nadu, India. It serves as the administrative headquarters of Dharmapuri district which is the first district created in Tamil Nadu after the independence of India by splitting it from then Salem district on 2 October 1965.

items.note: Laligam is a village panchayat in the Dharmapuri district, Tamil Nadu, India. As of 2011 Laligam had a population of 7,601 persons.

Poolapatti, is a village also officially spelled as Poolappatti, is a village located 6 km east from the major town Nallampalli, Tamil Nadu, India. This village comes under the Mittareddihalli (M.Reddihalli) Gram panchayat.
The Saint Francis Xavier Church in Kovilur, Dharamapuri is the oldest parish church in the diocese. It is called the mother church and the first Parish in the entire diocese.
Kovilur is a small Village in Nallampalli Block in Dharmapuri district of Tamil Nadu State, India. It comes under Nallampalli Panchayath.

Chenraya Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple located in the town of Adhiyamankottam, 7 km from Dharmapuri on the Salem road. The temple is dedicated to Vishnu and is believed to have been constructed by Hoysala and Vijayanagar kings, the most prominent being Krishnadevaraya.

The Diocese of Dharmapuri (Latin: Dharmapurien(sis)) is a Latin diocese of the Catholic Church located in the city of Dharmapuri, India, in the ecclesiastical province of Pondicherry and Cuddalore. The diocese administers to the political region of Dharmapuri district and Krishnagiri district.
Nallampalli is a Census town in Dharmapuri district in Tamil Nadu, India. It is the headquarters of Nallampalli taluk.

Dharmapuri is a town in the north western part of Tamil Nadu, India. It serves as the administrative headquarters of Dharmapuri district which is the first district created in Tamil Nadu after the independence of India by splitting it from then Salem district on 2 October 1965.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tangrakottai?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tangrakottai is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (135 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (119 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tangrakottai?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tangrakottai at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tangrakottai?
faq.a: Stays in Tangrakottai start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tangrakottai?
faq.a: The top attractions are Laligam, Poolapatti, St. Francis Xavier Church, Kovilur, Kovilur, Dharmapuri, Chenraya Perumal Temple.
